LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT REMEDY TEMPORARY SERVICES, LLC'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDIVIDUAL ARBITRATION, DISMISSAL OF PLAINTIFF'S CLASS CLAIMS, BIFURCATION OF PLAINTIFF'S PAGA CLAIM AND STAY OF PROCEEDINGS IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANT MOVING PARTY ENTERED INTO A BINDING AND ENFORCEABLE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT ("ARBITRATATION AGREEMENT") THAT INCLUDED A CLASS ACTION WAIVER PROVISION. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT COVERS THE FAILURE TO TIMELY PAY FINAL WAGES UPON TERMINATION CLAIM THAT IS AT ISSUE IN PLAINTIFF'S LAWSUIT. THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS NOT UNCONSCIONABLE. THE AAA RULES COMPORT WITH THE MINIMUM REQUIREMENTS SET FORTH IN ARMENDARIZ V. FOUNDATION HEALTH PSYCHCARE SERVICES, INC. (2000) 24 CAL.4TH 83. PLAINTIFF YOUNG'S VAGUE DECLARATION IS INSUFFICIENT TO ESTABLISH UNCONSCIONABILITY. THE CLASS ACTION WAIVER IN THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT IS ENFORCEABLE. (SEE ISKANIAN V. CLS TRANSPORTATION LOS ANGELES, LLC (2014) 59 CAL. 4TH 348.) PURSUANT TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT, THE FIRST AND SECOND CAUSES OF ACTION SHALL BE RESOLVED THROUGH INDIVIDUAL ARBITRATION. THE CLASS CLAIMS ARE DISMISSED PURSUANT TO THE ARBITRATION AGREEMENT'S CLASS ACTION WAIVER PROVISION. PLAINTIFF'S REPRESENTATIVE CLAIM PURSUANT TO THE PRIVATE ATORNEYS GENERAL ACT OF 2004 ("PAGA") SHALL BE STAYED PENDING THE COMPLETION OF ARBITRATION. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE, FILED AUGUST 15, 2014 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IN SUPPORT OF ITS REPLY BRIEF IS DENIED. TO THE EXTENT ADMISSIBILITY IS SOUGHT PURSUANT TO EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(D), THE DOCUMENTS ARE NOT RELEVANT. FURTHER, THE DOCUMENTS DO NOT MEET THE REQUIREMENTS OF EVIDENCE CODE SECTION 452(H), THE ALTERNATIVE BASIS FOR ADMISSIBILITY. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER; CLERK: S. KANE; NOT REPORTED.(302/MJM)

MINI MINUTES:
Defendant Remedy Temporary Services, LLC's Motion To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal Of Plaitniff'S Class Claims, Bifurcation Of Plaintiff'S PAGA Claim, And Stay Of Proceedings is ordered Off Calendar as a duplicative entry. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: S. Kane; Not reported.

Amended Notice Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal Of Pltff'S Class Claims, Bifurcation Of Pltff'S Paga Claim And Stay Of Proceedings
Off Calendar Oct-17-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances made by parties.

Court adopts its tentative ruling as to Defendant Remedy Temporary Services, LLC's Motion to Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal of Plaintiff's Class Claims, Bifurcation of Plaintiff's PAGA Claim and Stay of Proceedings. Defendant Remedy Temporary Services, LLC's Motion to Compel Individual Arbitration, Dismissal of Plaintiff's Class Claims, Bifurcation of Plaintiff's PAGA Claim and Stay of Proceedings is GRANTED. Plaintiff and Defendant moving party entered into a binding and enforceable arbitration agreement ("Arbitratation Agreement") that included a class action waiver provision. The Arbitration Agreement covers the failure to timely pay final wages upon termination claim that is at issue in Plaintiff's lawsuit. The Arbitration Agreement is not unconscionable. The AAA rules comport with the minimum requirements set forth in Armendariz v. Foundation Health Psychcare Services, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 83. Plaintiff Young's vague declaration is insufficient to establish unconscionability. The class action waiver in the Arbitration Agreement is enforceable. (See Iskanian v. CLS Transportation Los Angeles, LLC (2014) 59 Cal. 4th 348.) Pursuant to the Arbitration Agreement, the first and second causes of action shall be resolved through individual arbitration. The class claims are dismissed pursuant to the arbitration agreement's class action waiver provision. Plaintiff's representative claim pursuant to the Private Atorneys General Act of 2004 ("PAGA") shall be stayed pending the completion of arbitration. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice, filed August 15, 2014 is granted. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice in Support of Its Reply Brief is denied. To the extent admissibility is sought pursuant to Evidence Code section 452(d), the documents are not relevant. Further, the documents do not meet the requirements of Evidence Code section 452(h), the alternative basis for admissibility. (Prevailing party to prepare a proposed order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: S. Kane; Not reported.
